items.title: Ex-U.S. Amb.: Premature Troop Withdrawal Could Leave Iraq Facing Security Vacuum
items.desc: The former US ambassador to Iraq told Kurdistan24 that any American troop withdrawal should be guided by security conditions, warning that a premature exit would not serve Iraq, the Kurdistan Region, or the United States.

items.title: World Cup 2026 third-place preview: Mbappe’s last chance for Golden Boot
items.desc: Shafaq News- Miami France and England meet in the 2026 World Cup third-place playoff in Miami at 12:00 a.m. Baghdad time on Sunday, with both sides seeking a response to semifinal disappointment. items.title: Al-Sulaymaniyah airport partially grounds flights, Erbil remains operational
items.desc: Shafaq News- Al-Sulaymaniyah/ Erbil Al-Sulaymaniyah International Airport suspended two flights to Istanbul and Amman, while other operations continued without disruption, amid several Iranian strikes reported across the Kurdistan Region, an airport official told Shafaq News on Saturday. items.title: Hemophilia treatment halts in Iraqi Kurdistan
items.desc: Shafaq News- Al-Sulaymaniyah Persistent shortages of life-saving medicines and clotting factor injections are putting hundreds of people with hemophilia in the Kurdistan Region at growing risk, a patients' advocacy group warned on Saturday, urging authorities to restore supplies as soon as possible. items.title: US clears seven Iraqi banks for dollar transactions
items.desc: Shafaq News- Washington/ Baghdad Seven Iraqi banks have been cleared to resume correspondent banking relationships in foreign currencies, after meeting compliance and governance requirements, which Prime Minister Ali Al-Zaidi on Saturday hailed as “an important step” in Iraq’s banking reform program. items.title: Rocket strike ignites forest fire in Al-Sulaymaniyah
items.desc: Shafaq News- Al-Sulaymaniyah A rocket strike set off a fire that burned about 500 dunams (50 hectares) of pasture and natural forest near Arbat in Al-Sulaymaniyah province of Iraqi Kurdistan, the Forest and Environment Police Directorate announced Saturday.
